title: Tephroseris frigida
taxonomy: Tephroseris frigida is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is native to the Himalayan region and is also found in other mountainous areas of Asia. The genus Tephroseris is closely related to the genus Cremanium and is often considered to be a part of the same group. However, the exact classification of Tephroseris frigida can vary depending on the source and the taxonomic system used. Some sources may classify it as a synonym of Cremanium or as a separate genus within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Tephroseris frigida, also known as the alpine fleabane or alpine everlasting, is used in traditional medicine for its supposed health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used to make infusions, which are believed to have a soothing effect on the body. Additionally, the plant's roots are used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Tephroseris frigida's medicinal uses.
